summary:
Deep audit this week uncovered the root cause of our traffic quality issues: 88% of clicks were coming from Google Search Partners (low-quality partner sites, not Google Search itself) and 89% from mobile. We leave this on when launching campaigns to get more data, but this was a specifically bad case of spam. 

 This means a majority of weekly spend was going to junk traffic. We've now turned off Search Partners and added -50% mobile bid adjustments (a lot of ad spend was going to mobile with zero conversions, makes sense with audience). I expect fewer clicks next week, but dramatically better quality. Looking to scale as soon as possible but we need 2-3 weeks of clean data before I can recommend scaling ad spend.

metrics.label: Ad Spend
metrics.note: Inflated by low-quality traffic sources — now resolved. Expect lower but cleaner spend next week.

metrics.label: Conversions
metrics.note: Down from 5 — last week's number included spam traffic.

metrics.label: Cost per Conversion
metrics.note: Up from $122.51 — transitional number, expect improvement as cleaner traffic kicks in.

metrics.label: Avg CPC
metrics.note: Up slightly from $1.09 — still strong for B2B SaaS.

funnel.title: Ad-to-Demo Funnel
funnel.notes: Single conversion from Integration Intent ($196.25). Competitor Conquest had 0 this week — may be related to spam reduction from broader negative keywords.

charts.title: Traffic Quality: Where Budget Was Going

findings.title: Search Partners Consuming 88% of Clicks
findings.description: The vast majority of traffic was coming from low-quality Google Search Partner sites rather than Google Search itself. This is a primary driver of spam and irrelevant traffic.

findings.title: Mobile Traffic at 89% With No Bid Adjustment
findings.description: Mobile users dominated spend without converting proportionally.

actions.title: Turned Off Search Partners Across All Campaigns
actions.description: Eliminated the #1 source of low-quality traffic. 88% of clicks were coming from partner sites instead of Google Search.

actions.title: Added -50% Mobile Bid Adjustment
actions.description: Reduced mobile bid by 50% across all 5 campaigns to shift budget toward desktop/tablet traffic which converts better for enterprise demos.

actions.title: API Keyword Angle Has No Search Demand
actions.description: Researched 'API for Events' differentiation idea. Best candidate 'event management api' has ~30 monthly US searches — not viable for paid or organic. Let's keep brainstorming new angles as we optimize.

nextSteps: Looking for 2-3 weeks of clean data collection before scaling for clean use of ad spend.
We'll monitor Week 4 performance to measure impact of Search Partners removal and mobile bid adjustment.
Reassess Event Registration campaign after one more week of cleaner traffic data.
Target mid-April for scaling readiness assessment with Saroosh based on verified lead costs.
